May 1st, 2026 ( Transcript Daily - Delivered by Newstex )

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, NOV, HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, and Clearway Energy are the five Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of companies that develop, build, operate, or supply technology and services for lowcarbon power sources such as solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and bioenergy, including equipment makers, project developers, and utilities with significant renewable portfolios. For investors, these stocks provide exposure to the global energy transition and are often viewed as growth or thematic investments whose returns are influenced by government policy, subsidies, project pipelines, technology costs, and sector-specific volatility. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services (PWR)

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group (WEC)

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

NOV (NOV)

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NOV

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI)

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

Clearway Energy (CWEN)

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CWEN

urn:contentItem:6JGG-0F73-RSW1-K1GW-00000-00Top Renewable Energy Stocks To Follow Today - May 1st2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:58ZLexisNexisTop Renewable Energy Stocks To Follow Today - May 1st

The Nasdaq's Historic Rally Doesn't Mean the Risk Is Gone

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, NOV, HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, and Clearway Energy are the five Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of publicly traded companies that generate electricity from renewable sources solar, wind, hydro, geothermal or make the equipment and services that support that sector panels, turbines, batteries, grid technology. For investors, they provide equity exposure to the growth and risks of the clean-energy transition, including policy shifts, technology development, and commodity or market volatility. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services PWR

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

WEC Energy Group WEC

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

NOV NOV

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital HASI

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Clearway Energy CWEN

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

value.Title: Top Renewable Energy Stocks To Follow Today - May 1st

urn:contentItem:6JGG-0F73-RSW1-K0V0-00000-00Top Renewable Energy Stocks To Follow Today - May 1st2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:58ZLexisNexisTop Renewable Energy Stocks To Follow Today - May 1st

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, NOV, HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, and Clearway Energy are the five Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of publicly traded companies that generate electricity from renewable sources solar, wind, hydro, geothermal or make the equipment and services that support that sector panels, turbines, batteries, grid technology. For investors, they provide equity exposure to the growth and risks of the clean-energy transition, including policy shifts, technology development, and commodity or market volatility. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services PWR

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group WEC

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

NOV NOV

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NOV

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital HASI

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

Clearway Energy CWEN

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CWEN

Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Mark Dietrich

April 30th, 2026 ( Watchlist News - Delivered by Newstex )

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, NOV, Clearway Energy, HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Clearway Energy, and Gibraltar Industries are the seven Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are equity shares of companies whose primary business is developing, generating, manufacturing equipment for, or providing services to renewable power sources (for example solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and energy storage). Investors buy them to gain exposure to the clean-energy transition, but these stocks can be more volatile and sensitive to government policy, commodity and interest-rate moves, technological change, and project-specific risks than broad-market equities. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services (PWR)

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group (WEC)

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

NOV (NOV)

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NOV

Clearway Energy (CWEN)

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CWEN

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI)

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

Clearway Energy (CWEN.A)

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CWEN.A

Gibraltar Industries (ROCK)

Gibraltar Industries, Inc. manufactures and provides products and services for the renewable energy, residential, agtech, and infrastructure markets in the United States and internationally. It operates through four segments: Renewables, Residential, Agtech, and Infrastructure. The Renewables segment designs, engineers, manufactures, and installs solar racking and electrical balance of systems for commercial and distributed generation scale solar installations.

Read Our Latest Research Report on ROCK

April 30th, 2026 ( DailyPolitical - Delivered by Newstex )

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, NOV, HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, and Clearway Energy are the five Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of publicly traded companies that generate electricity from renewable sources (solar, wind, hydro, geothermal) or make the equipment and services that support that sector (panels, turbines, batteries, grid technology). For investors, they provide equity exposure to the growth and risks of the clean-energy transition, including policy shifts, technology development, and commodity or market volatility. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services (PWR)

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group (WEC)

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

NOV (NOV)

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NOV

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI)

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

Clearway Energy (CWEN)

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CWEN

Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

April 30th, 2026 ( Ticker Report - Delivered by Newstex )

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, NOV, HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, and Clearway Energy are the five Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of publicly traded companies that generate electricity from renewable sources (solar, wind, hydro, geothermal) or make the equipment and services that support that sector (panels, turbines, batteries, grid technology). For investors, they provide equity exposure to the growth and risks of the clean-energy transition, including policy shifts, technology development, and commodity or market volatility. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services (PWR)

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group (WEC)

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

NOV (NOV)

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NOV

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI)

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

Clearway Energy (CWEN)

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CWEN

value.Title: Grow Solar Program Returns

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGB-FKH3-SD6M-J47N-00000-00Grow Solar Program Returns2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:58ZLexisNexisNEWSPg. 24Grow Solar Program ReturnsMichael TrogeONEIDA ENGINEERING DEPARTMENT ENERGY PROJECT MANAGER

In 2025, Oneida Nation, City of Green Bay, and the Midwest Renewable Energy Association (MREA) introduced the Grow Solar Program (GSP) to Outagamie, Brown, and Winnebago Counties. The Program is opening back up in 2026. This was a volume discount program, which means that the more kilowatts purchased throughout the region, the greater the rebate the installer would apply to the final cost. This can add up to about $500 to $1000 of reduced cost to your system. The Investment Tax Credit (ITC) for residential solar is no longer around. For residential installs, the GSP and the increased Focus on Energy incentive still make solar an opportunity.

ATTENTION BUSINESSES, NONPROFITS, AND GOVERNMENTS:

The ITC is still available until the end of 2027.

This is the best time to act! A meeting is scheduled at the VFW, 2980 East Service Road, Oneida at 5pm Wednesday, May 6, 2026, to describe the program and schedule a site assessment.

urn:contentItem:6JK4-0TW3-S3VK-N4BS-00000-00Renewable Energy Stocks To Keep An Eye On - May 12th2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:58ZLexisNexisRenewable Energy Stocks To Keep An Eye On - May 12th

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, NOV, Clearway Energy, HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Enlight Renewable Energy, and Gibraltar Industries are the seven Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of companies involved in generating, developing, or supporting energy sources that can be naturally replenished, such as solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and biomass. For stock market investors, these stocks offer a way to gain exposure to the clean energy sector and its growth potential, but they can also be more volatile because they depend on factors like government policy, technology costs, and energy prices. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services PWR

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

WEC Energy Group WEC

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

NOV NOV

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Clearway Energy CWEN

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital HASI

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Enlight Renewable Energy ENLT

Enlight Renewable Energy Ltd operates a renewable energy platform in Israel, Central-EasternEurope, Western Europe, and the United States. The company develops, finances, constructs, owns, and operates utility-scale renewable energy projects. It develops wind energy and solar energy projects, as well as energy storage projects.

Gibraltar Industries ROCK

Gibraltar Industries, Inc. manufactures and provides products and services for the renewable energy, residential, agtech, and infrastructure markets in the United States and internationally. It operates through four segments: Renewables, Residential, Agtech, and Infrastructure. The Renewables segment designs, engineers, manufactures, and installs solar racking and electrical balance of systems for commercial and distributed generation scale solar installations.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

WEC Energy Group, Inc., a Fortune 500 company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading energy services provider in the United States, primarily operating in Wisconsin and surrounding regions. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, along with natural gas delivery, serving over 4.6 million customers through subsidiaries like We Energies and Wisconsin Public Service. The company focuses on reliable, affordable, and sustainable energy solutions, with a diversified portfolio that includes fossil fuels, nuclear, and renewables. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean power, supportive regulations, and corporate sustainability goals. This growth is evidenced by significant investments in wind farms and solar installations, positioning WEC Energy to meet rising environmental standards while enhancing long-term operational efficiency and shareholder value. It is the NYSE's 7th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

WEC Energy Group, Inc, listed on the NYSE as WEC, plays a pivotal role in ensuring efficient data center power supply WEC Energy, focusing on enhancing data center interconnection services WEC and providing data center high-capacity service WEC. Driven by their capital expenditure plans, WEC Energy Group capex risk is strategically managed with investments in grid modernization WEC Energy, including transmission upgrades WEC, distribution network upgrades WEC, and substation upgrades WEC, all contributing to WEC rate base growth. As a regulated utility electricity WEC provider, WEC services large commercial and industrial power WEC needs with firm power agreements WEC ensuring reliability and resiliency WEC.

Innovations such as battery energy storage WEC, renewable energy integration WEC, on-site generation and CHP WEC, and EV charging infrastructure WEC are prominent. Demand response programs WEC, smart grid AMI WEC, and outage management WEC are integral to We Energies commercial service. Wisconsin Public Service data center service, Peoples Gas industrial gas supply, and North Shore Gas commercial solutions are part of their diverse offerings. Infrastructure projects WEC Energy, regulatory rate cases WEC, and long-term contracts for data centers WEC are monitored closely, addressing investor capex concerns WEC and shaping the capital spending outlook WEC Energy.

SECTION 3 RECENT NEWS, PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES & PAST MONTH ECONOMIC NEWS

3.1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 439 words

Thu Apr 9, 2026: WEC Energy Group's Strategic Focus on Data Centers and Capital Expenditure

WEC Energy Group, Inc. continues to draw attention as analysts refine their price targets in light of emerging data center demands and a substantial capital expenditure (capex) plan. On 9th April 2026, the internal fair value model for WEC Energy remains stable at US$123.09 per share, indicating a consistent valuation anchor even as market dynamics evolve. Recent adjustments in price targets are largely motivated by insights into earnings visibility and the company's ambitious US$37.5 billion capex strategy, which is poised to support an increasing load from hyperscale data centers.

Support for WEC Energy's growth narrative has been highlighted by KeyBanc, which recently increased its price target to US$126 from US$117 following a solid fourth-quarter performance. Analyst Sophie Karp noted that the company's adjusted earnings per share surpassed expectations, and the reaffirmation of fiscal year 2026 guidance reinforces confidence in WEC Energy's long-term trajectory. KeyBanc's report emphasizes the crucial link between the capex initiatives and the anticipated growth stemming from data center demands, suggesting a robust outlook for the company in the coming years.

Conversely, some analysts express caution regarding WEC Energy's valuation amidst the expansive spending plans. RBC Capital revised its price target downwards in January 2026, while Wells Fargo followed suit, citing concerns about the execution of the increased capex program. This divergence in analyst sentiment, as reflected in revisions from firms such as Barclays and Mizuho, indicates a lack of consensus on how much of the data center opportunity and associated capex risks are currently priced into WEC Energy shares. Investors may find it challenging to gauge the overall sentiment as they weigh both bullish and bearish perspectives.

In addition to market reactions, WEC Energy Group is preparing for a significant corporate governance shift, with a shareholder vote scheduled for 7th May 2026 to amend its Articles of Incorporation and Bylaws. The proposed changes would eliminate supermajority voting requirements, potentially streamlining decision-making processes. Furthermore, the recent acknowledgment by the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission of the Sandstone Rapids Hydroelectric Project's assessment report signals regulatory progress, which may also influence the company's strategic direction and operational efficiencies.

As WEC Energy Group navigates these evolving dynamics, maintaining a focus on grid modernization, renewable energy projects, and innovations in operational efficiency remain paramount. The company is utilizing OneLayer Bridge to improve its management of cellular-connected devices, aiming to enhance service resiliency and efficiency in electricity and natural gas delivery. urn:contentItem:6JK1-99W3-S11V-T2GM-00000-00Renewable Energy Stocks To Keep An Eye On - May 12th2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:58ZLexisNexisRenewable Energy Stocks To Keep An Eye On - May 12th

Accordingly, no warranties or other guarantees are offered as to the quality of the opinions, commentary or anything else appearing in such Newstex Authoritative Content. Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Nicole Kennedy

May 12th, 2026 ( Watchlist News - Delivered by Newstex )

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, NOV, Clearway Energy, HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Enlight Renewable Energy, and Gibraltar Industries are the seven Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of companies involved in generating, developing, or supporting energy sources that can be naturally replenished, such as solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and biomass. For stock market investors, these stocks offer a way to gain exposure to the clean energy sector and its growth potential, but they can also be more volatile because they depend on factors like government policy, technology costs, and energy prices. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services (PWR)

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group (WEC)

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

NOV (NOV)

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NOV

Clearway Energy (CWEN)

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CWEN

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI)

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

Enlight Renewable Energy (ENLT)

Enlight Renewable Energy Ltd operates a renewable energy platform in Israel, Central-EasternEurope, Western Europe, and the United States. The company develops, finances, constructs, owns, and operates utility-scale renewable energy projects. It develops wind energy and solar energy projects, as well as energy storage projects.

Read Our Latest Research Report on ENLT

Gibraltar Industries (ROCK)

Gibraltar Industries, Inc. manufactures and provides products and services for the renewable energy, residential, agtech, and infrastructure markets in the United States and internationally. It operates through four segments: Renewables, Residential, Agtech, and Infrastructure. The Renewables segment designs, engineers, manufactures, and installs solar racking and electrical balance of systems for commercial and distributed generation scale solar installations.

Read Our Latest Research Report on ROCK

value.Title: Top Renewable Energy Stocks To Keep An Eye On - May 11th

May 11th, 2026 ( DailyPolitical - Delivered by Newstex )

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, NOV, Enlight Renewable Energy, and Clearway Energy are the five Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of companies that develop, produce, or support energy sources like solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and other low-carbon technologies. For stock market investors, these stocks offer a way to invest in the transition to cleaner energy, but they can also be volatile because their performance depends on factors like government policy, energy prices, technology costs, and demand growth. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services (PWR)

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group (WEC)

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

NOV (NOV)

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NOV

Enlight Renewable Energy (ENLT)

Enlight Renewable Energy Ltd operates a renewable energy platform in Israel, Central-Eastern Europe, Western Europe, and the United States. The company develops, finances, constructs, owns, and operates utility-scale renewable energy projects. It develops wind energy and solar energy projects, as well as energy storage projects.

Read Our Latest Research Report on ENLT

Clearway Energy (CWEN)

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CWEN

May 10th, 2026 ( Transcript Daily - Delivered by Newstex )

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, NOV, Enlight Renewable Energy, and Clearway Energy are the five Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of companies that develop, produce, or support energy sources like solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and other low-carbon technologies. For stock market investors, these stocks offer a way to invest in the transition to cleaner energy, but they can also be volatile because their performance depends on factors like government policy, energy prices, technology costs, and demand growth. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services (PWR)

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group (WEC)

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

NOV (NOV)

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NOV

Enlight Renewable Energy (ENLT)

Enlight Renewable Energy Ltd operates a renewable energy platform in Israel, Central-EasternEurope, Western Europe, and the United States. The company develops, finances, constructs, owns, and operates utility-scale renewable energy projects. It develops wind energy and solar energy projects, as well as energy storage projects.

Read Our Latest Research Report on ENLT

Clearway Energy (CWEN)

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CWEN

urn:contentItem:6JJG-41B3-S7X0-W1TJ-00000-00Renewable Energy Stocks To Watch Now - May 9th2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:58ZLexisNexisRenewable Energy Stocks To Watch Now - May 9th

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, NOV, and Clearway Energy are the five Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of publicly traded companies involved in producing, developing, or supporting energy sources like solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and other low-carbon technologies. For stock market investors, these stocks offer a way to gain exposure to the growth of the clean energy sector, though their prices can be influenced by factors such as government policy, energy demand, technology costs, and market volatility. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services PWR

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

WEC Energy Group WEC

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital HASI

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

NOV NOV

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NOV

Clearway Energy CWEN

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

value.Title: Renewable Energy Stocks To Watch Now - May 9th

May 9th, 2026 ( Zolmax.com - Delivered by Newstex )

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, NOV, and Clearway Energy are the five Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of publicly traded companies involved in producing, developing, or supporting energy sources like solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and other low-carbon technologies. For stock market investors, these stocks offer a way to gain exposure to the growth of the clean energy sector, though their prices can be influenced by factors such as government policy, energy demand, technology costs, and market volatility. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services (PWR)

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group (WEC)

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI)

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

NOV (NOV)

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NOV

Clearway Energy (CWEN)

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CWEN

urn:contentItem:6JJ3-26S3-S1B9-30NC-00000-00Renewable Energy Stocks To Watch Now - May 9th2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:58ZLexisNexisRenewable Energy Stocks To Watch Now - May 9th

The Newstex Authoritative Content shall be construed as author-based content and commentary. Accordingly, no warranties or other guarantees are offered as to the quality of the opinions, commentary or anything else appearing in such Newstex Authoritative Content. Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

May 8th, 2026 ( Ticker Report - Delivered by Newstex )

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, NOV, and Clearway Energy are the five Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of publicly traded companies involved in producing, developing, or supporting energy sources like solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and other low-carbon technologies. For stock market investors, these stocks offer a way to gain exposure to the growth of the clean energy sector, though their prices can be influenced by factors such as government policy, energy demand, technology costs, and market volatility. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services (PWR)

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group (WEC)

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI)

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

NOV (NOV)

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NOV

Clearway Energy (CWEN)

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CWEN

value.Title: Renewable Energy Stocks To Watch Now - May 9th

urn:contentItem:6JJ5-R4V3-RTC7-2506-00000-00Renewable Energy Stocks To Watch Now - May 9th2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:58ZLexisNexisRenewable Energy Stocks To Watch Now - May 9th

The Nasdaq's Historic Rally Doesn't Mean the Risk Is Gone

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, NOV, and Clearway Energy are the five Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of publicly traded companies involved in producing, developing, or supporting energy sources like solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and other low-carbon technologies. For stock market investors, these stocks offer a way to gain exposure to the growth of the clean energy sector, though their prices can be influenced by factors such as government policy, energy demand, technology costs, and market volatility. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services PWR

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

The Power Bill, the AI Dip, and the Date That Could Flip 2026 Stocks

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group WEC

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Quanta Services: The Backbone of the AI Data Center Push

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital HASI

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

NOV NOV

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NOV

Clearway Energy CWEN

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CWEN

value.Title: Promising Renewable Energy Stocks To Follow Now - May 7th

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ2-5MJ3-RW76-P22B-00000-00Promising Renewable Energy Stocks To Follow Now - May 7th2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:58ZLexisNexisPromising Renewable Energy Stocks To Follow Now - May 7th

Quanta Services, NOV, WEC Energy Group, HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, and Clearway Energy are the five Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of publicly traded companies whose primary business involves producing renewable power like wind, solar, hydro or supplying technologies and services that support the clean-energy transition such as battery manufacturers, turbine makers, or installers. For investors, these stocks offer a way to gain exposure to the growth and policy-driven demand for low-carbon energy, but they can be volatile and are sensitive to technology cycles, government incentives, and commodity or interest-rate shifts. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services PWR

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

NOV NOV

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NOV

WEC Energy Group WEC

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital HASI

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

Clearway Energy CWEN

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CWEN

May 8th, 2026 ( DailyPolitical - Delivered by Newstex )

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, NOV, and Clearway Energy are the five Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of publicly traded companies involved in producing, developing, or supporting energy sources like solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and other low-carbon technologies. For stock market investors, these stocks offer a way to gain exposure to the growth of the clean energy sector, though their prices can be influenced by factors such as government policy, energy demand, technology costs, and market volatility. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services (PWR)

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group (WEC)

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI)

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

NOV (NOV)

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NOV

Clearway Energy (CWEN)

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CWEN

urn:contentItem:6JHV-7833-RTBG-P3F4-00000-00Renewable Energy Stocks To Follow Today - May 6th2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:58ZLexisNexisRenewable Energy Stocks To Follow Today - May 6th

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, NOV, Clearway Energy, and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital are the five Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of publicly traded companies whose core businesses involve producing, manufacturing equipment for, developing projects in, or financing clean power sources-such as solar, wind, hydro, biomass, energy storage, and related technologies and services. Investors use them to gain exposure to the shift away from fossil fuels; their returns are influenced by technology costs, government policy and incentives, energy demand and commodity prices, and can be more volatile than broader-market holdings. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services PWR

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. urn:contentItem:6JHR-N013-S227-V4M8-00000-00Renewable Energy Stocks To Follow Today - May 6th2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:58ZLexisNexisRenewable Energy Stocks To Follow Today - May 6th

May 6th, 2026 ( Transcript Daily - Delivered by Newstex )

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, NOV, Clearway Energy, and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital are the five Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of publicly traded companies whose core businesses involve producing, manufacturing equipment for, developing projects in, or financing clean power sourcessuch as solar, wind, hydro, biomass, energy storage, and related technologies and services. Investors use them to gain exposure to the shift away from fossil fuels; their returns are influenced by technology costs, government policy and incentives, energy demand and commodity prices, and can be more volatile than broader-market holdings. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services (PWR)

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group (WEC)

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

NOV (NOV)

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NOV

Clearway Energy (CWEN)

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CWEN

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI)

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

urn:contentItem:6JHH-VF93-SFSY-016G-00000-00Renewable Energy Stocks To Follow Today - May 6th2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:58ZLexisNexisRenewable Energy Stocks To Follow Today - May 6th

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, NOV, Clearway Energy, and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital are the five Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of publicly traded companies whose core businesses involve producing, manufacturing equipment for, developing projects in, or financing clean power sources-such as solar, wind, hydro, biomass, energy storage, and related technologies and services. Investors use them to gain exposure to the shift away from fossil fuels; their returns are influenced by technology costs, government policy and incentives, energy demand and commodity prices, and can be more volatile than broader-market holdings. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services PWR

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

WEC Energy Group WEC

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

NOV NOV

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Clearway Energy CWEN

Clearway Energy, Inc. operates in the renewable energy business in the United States. The company operates through Conventional and Renewables segments. It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital HASI

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. May 6th, 2026 ( Zolmax.com - Delivered by Newstex )

Quanta Services, NOV, WEC Energy Group, HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, and Clearway Energy are the five Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of publicly traded companies whose primary business involves producing renewable power (like wind, solar, hydro) or supplying technologies and services that support the clean-energy transition (such as battery manufacturers, turbine makers, or installers). For investors, these stocks offer a way to gain exposure to the growth and policy-driven demand for low-carbon energy, but they can be volatile and are sensitive to technology cycles, government incentives, and commodity or interest-rate shifts. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services (PWR)

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

NOV (NOV)

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

It has approximately 6,000 net MW of installed wind, solar, and energy generation projects; and approximately 2,500 net MW of natural gas-fired generation facilities.
